Off Calendar Apr-02-2025 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Jan-14-2025 Continued To Apr-02-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jackson Junliang Chen, Administrator in Propria Persona (via Zoom).

Mr. Chen reported that an inventory and appraisal was submitted to "appraiser" Shirley Yawitz on November 28, 2024. She is still working on one of the listed assets and has not provided an estimated time for completion. Mr. Chen stated he had followed up with Ms. Yawitz two weeks prior to the hearing but has not received any further updates.

The court continued the matter to April 2, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204 for further updates.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Issuance Of Letters
Off Calendar Sep-17-2024 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Issuance Of Letters
Off Calendar Aug-06-2024 Continued To Sep-17-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jackson Chen appeared by zoom in propria persona.

Mr. Chen stated that the letters have not been issued because he does not know how to acquire them. The court informed Mr. Chen that if he wants to remain as the administrator, he will has to follow the rules. The clerk advised Mr. Chen to call the filing department and speak with the clerk that rejected the letters. Status on the issuance of letters is continued to September 17, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Belen. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-03-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jackson Chen appeared by zoom in propria persona.

The court asked Mr. Chen why he needed to open this probate case. Mr. Chen stated that the decedent died without a will, that the estate has more than the amount needed to not have to open probate, and that there is about $200,000.00 to $300,000.00 in retirement funds, cash, savings and a property in the East Coast that he believes is part of this estate. The court stated that there a lot things that Mr. Chen will need to do and suggested getting an attorney to help him. Mr. Chen stated that he has a few more steps, that the parents are on the same page and that there is no conflict between them. The Petition for Letters of Administration is under submission with the court's intent to grant. The notice defects are waived. The court informed Mr. Chen that in the future he has to provide notice of how to make remote appearances. Status on the issuance of letters is set for August 6, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal is set for January 14, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A status hearing for filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for September 9, 2025, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Bond is waived.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported.
